payload.text: Build an interactive map-based chatbot that uses real-time Google Maps data to answer location-specific questions.

**Defining the Objective**

I'm currently focused on defining the project objective: crafting an interactive, map-based chatbot. The core functionality hinges on leveraging real-time Google Maps data through Gemini's grounding capabilities to respond to location-specific queries.


**Integrating the Design**

I'm now integrating the provided HTML/Tailwind/GSAP website design into a React structure. I'm focusing on preserving the original aesthetic while incorporating the chatbot functionality. This involves breaking the HTML into React components like `Hero`, `Services`, `About`, and others to match the landing page's look, and introducing components for the chatbot interface (`ChatWidget`) and map panel (`MapPanel`).


**Visualizing Map Data**

I've realized that simply displaying text from the grounding tool isn't truly "map-based." To enhance interactivity without extra API keys, I'll integrate a split-view or modal setup. This will clearly highlight the "Grounding Chunks" (map links/snippets) returned by Gemini alongside the chatbot interface. The chatbot will be the centerpiece.


**Outlining Key Features**

I'm now outlining key features. Given the landing page and "SOTIC" context, the chatbot will be focused on location-based queries using the `googleMaps` tool. I'm now thinking the chatbot will be either a floating widget or a dedicated section within the page.

**Implementing Core React Structure**

I am moving onto implementing the core React structure. I'm currently setting up the Tailwind/GSAP integration and porting the provided HTML landing page into `App.tsx` and sub-components. I'm thinking of sticking to `App.tsx` plus a few key components to keep things efficient.



**Prioritizing Gemini Model**

I've determined I must use `gemini-2.5-flash` for the map features, as Maps grounding is only supported in that series. This is critical for location-based queries using the `googleMaps` tool, despite the mention of `gemini-3-flash-preview` and search. I'm focusing on a flexible service, prioritizing the Gemini 2.5 series when dealing with location-based requests to ensure real-time Google Maps data integration.


**Refining the Implementation**

I'm now diving into the code, and I'm self-correcting my approach. I'm focusing on geolocation with `navigator.geolocation` for accurate map queries. I am adding `lucide-react` for icons and considering `useLayoutEffect` to handle GSAP animations. I'm also preparing key files like `metadata.json`, `index.html`, and `App.tsx` for the React structure. I am working on the chatbot, and on the map display. I'll need to show "Map Locations" (Links to Google Maps). I'm focusing on recreating the original dark/neon design with Tailwind, preserving its `glass` classes, gradients, and typography.


**Developing API Calls**

I'm now detailing `geminiService.ts`: the new `sendMessageToGemini` function will handle API calls. It will take location data and configure Gemini with `gemini-2.5-flash` and `googleMaps` (and `googleSearch`) tools. The function will extract and handle text and grounding data from the API response. I am focusing on making the tool function as I expect, with all the necessary outputs.

\n\n

I'm now implementing `ChatWidget.tsx` with states for `isOpen`, `messages`, `input`, and `userLocation`. I am preparing to fetch the current position on mount, and render chat bubbles and map location cards. My focus is on rendering chat bubbles, and displaying cards for grounding metadata, with map locations or search results where appropriate.

**Troubleshooting GitHub Connection**

I'm currently focused on the GitHub connection issue. It seems like the user's facing a general technical hurdle, not something specific to the SOTIC AI assistant app's code itself. My priority is to understand the scope of the problem – is it account-specific, or more widespread? I am working on determining the parameters of this technical problem.

Para resolver problemas de conexão com o GitHub, geralmente o erro está relacionado à autenticação ou rede. Aqui estão os passos principais para diagnosticar e corrigir:

### 1. Verifique a Autenticação (Método Recomendado)
O GitHub não aceita mais senhas comuns para operações via linha de comando.
*   **Token de Acesso Pessoal (PAT):** Se você usa HTTPS, deve gerar um token em *Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens* e usá-lo no lugar da senha.
*   **SSH:** Se preferir SSH, verifique se sua chave pública está cadastrada no GitHub:
    ```bash
    ssh -T git@github.com
    ```
    Se aparecer "Hi [seu-usuario]! You've successfully authenticated", a conexão está funcionando.

### 2. Erros de Proxy ou Firewall
Se você estiver em uma rede corporativa, pode precisar configurar o proxy:
```bash
git config --global http.proxy http://usuario:senha@proxy.server.com:porta
```

### 3. Verifique a URL do Repositório
Garanta que você está tentando se conectar ao endereço correto:
```bash
git remote -v
```
Se estiver errado, corrija com:
```bash
git remote set-url origin https://github.com/usuario/repositorio.git
```

### 4. Teste a Conexão de Rede
Tente dar um `ping` ou acessar o site pelo navegador. Se o site abre mas o Git não funciona, o problema é local na configuração do Git ou na sua chave SSH/Token.

**Qual é a mensagem de erro específica que aparece no seu terminal?** Com o erro exato (ex: "Permission denied", "Connection timed out"), posso te dar a solução precisa.

**Troubleshooting GitHub Login**

I'm currently dealing with a frustrating "Sync to GitHub" error. The visual indicates a failed login attempt, displaying a Portuguese error message about something going wrong. I've re-attempted the login, but the error persists. It is not clear whether the issue is client-side or server-side, but it seems to prevent any further action. I am now trying to deduce the root cause of the authentication failure.

Esse erro geralmente ocorre quando a sessão de autenticação com o GitHub expirou ou houve uma falha no handshake do OAuth. Para resolver, siga estes passos:

1.  **Tente Novamente:** Clique no botão **"Fazer login no GitHub para continuar"**. Às vezes, uma segunda tentativa resolve problemas temporários de rede.
2.  **Janela Anônima/Cookies:** Se você estiver usando uma janela anônima ou tiver bloqueado "cookies de terceiros", o login do GitHub pode falhar. Certifique-se de que os cookies estão permitidos para o site.
3.  **Limpar Cache:** Tente limpar o cache e os cookies do navegador especificamente para `github.com` e para a plataforma que você está usando agora.
4.  **Verifique extensões:** Bloqueadores de anúncios (AdBlockers) ou extensões de segurança podem interferir no pop-up de login. Tente desativá-los temporariamente.
5.  **Revogar e Autorizar:** Se o problema persistir, vá até as configurações do seu GitHub (*Settings > Applications > Authorized OAuth Apps*), remova a permissão da plataforma e tente fazer o login aqui novamente para forçar uma nova autorização.

Se nada disso funcionar, verifique se o GitHub não está passando por uma instabilidade momentânea em [githubstatus.com](https://www.githubstatus.com/).
